Given some categorized constants in the:
```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
clients("Aimee"; "Ginger"; "Freda"; "Hannah").
price(150; 160; 170; 180).
masseuse("Lynda"; "Nancy"; "Teri"; "Whitney").

% For each client, there is exactly one masseuse and one price assigned.
{schedule(Client, Masseuse, Price): masseuse(Masseuse), price(Price)}=1 :- clients(Client).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
diplomats("Gilbert"; "Macdonald"; "Pickett"; "Vasquez").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
duration(4; 5; 9; 10).

% For each diplomat D, assign exactly 1 month M and 1 duration T.
{assign(D, M, T): months(M), duration(T)}=1 :- diplomats(D).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
names("Cornick"; "Dreadco"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).
companies("Permias"; "Rubicorp"; "Techtrin"; "Ubersplore").

% For each name C, it launches exactly 1 company R and 1 month M.
{launch(C, R, M): companies(R), months(M)}=1 :- names(C).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
witnesses("Benny Baron"; "Edith Estes"; "Hal Harrison"; "Iva Ingram").
dates(4; 5; 6; 7).
towns("Islesboro"; "Long Barn"; "Tarzana"; "Zearing").

% For each witness W, it is associated with exactly one date D and one town T.
{sighting(W, D, T): dates(D), towns(T)}=1 :- witnesses(W).
```

```asp
age(4; 5; 8; 9).

% For each name, it matches with exactly 1 age and 1 wingspan.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
games("Angry Ants"; "Bubble Bombs"; "Flowcarts"; "Peter Parka"; "Wizard World").
months(3; 4; 5; 6; 7).
developers("Apptastic"; "Digibits"; "Gadingo"; "Novio"; "Vortia").

% For each game, it is released in exactly 1 month and with exactly 1 developer.
{released(Game, Month, Developer): months(Month), developers(Developer)}=1 :- games(Game).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
vintage(1984; 1988; 1992; 1996).
wine("Annata Branco"; "Friambliss"; "Luzagueil"; "Zifennwein").
type("chardonnay"; "merlot"; "pinot gris"; "syrah").

% For each wine W, it matches with exactly 1 vintage V and 1 type T.
{match(W, V, T): vintage(V), type(T)}=1 :- wine(W).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
witnesses("Benny Baron"; "Dan Delgado"; "Edith Estes"; "Gil Gates").
dates(4; 5; 6; 7).
towns("Crescent City"; "Embden"; "Islesboro"; "Walnut Creek").

% Define the relation: sighting(W, D, T) means witness W was seen on date D in town T.
% We generate all possible combinations of witness, date, and town.
sighting(W, D, T) :- witnesses(W), dates(D), towns(T).

% Enforce the constraint: Benny Baron was seen 2 days after Dan Delgado.
% That is, D1 = D2 + 2, where D1 is Benny's date and D2 is Dan's date.
:- sighting("Benny Baron", D1, _), sighting("Dan Delgado", D2, _), D1 != D2 + 2.
```

```asp
distance(525; 550; 575; 600).

% For each car Car, it is associated with exactly one speed Speed and one distance Distance.
{car_info(Car, Speed, Distance): speed(Speed), distance(Distance)}=1 :- cars(Car).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.

height(100; 105; 110; 115).
country("Brazil"; "Nigeria"; "Tibet"; "Switzerland").

% For each waterfall W, it is located at exactly 1 height H and in exactly 1 country C.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
packsize(25; 30; 35; 40).
brands("Adironda"; "Grennel"; "Naturba"; "Travelore").
names("Arthur"; "Eugene"; "Natasha"; "Olga").

% Each name is assigned exactly one packsize and one brand.
{assign(N, S, B) : packsize(S), brands(B)} = 1 :- names(N).

% Clue 1: Arthur's pack is either the 25 liter pack or the Adironda pack.
:- assign("Arthur", S, B), S != 25, B != "Adironda".

% Clue 2: Eugene's pack is made by Adironda.
:- assign("Eugene", S, B), B != "Adironda".

% Clue 3: Natasha's pack is either the 40 liter pack or the Travelore pack.
:- assign("Natasha", S, B), S != 40, B != "Travelore".

% Clue 4: Natasha's pack is 10 liters larger than the Grennel pack.
:- assign("Natasha", S_N, _), assign(N, S_G, "Grennel"), S_N != S_G + 10.

% Clue 5: The 30 liter pack is made by Grennel.
:- assign(N, S, B), S = 30, B != "Grennel".
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
```
```asp
players("Awad"; "Daregh"; "Gardelli"; "Rothvum").
years(1976; 1979; 1982; 1985).
positions("center back"; "center forward"; "goalie"; "sweeper").

% Define the main relation: each player is assigned exactly one year and one position.
{player_year_position(P, Y, Pos): years(Y), positions(Pos)}=1 :- players(P).

% Ensure each year is used exactly once (bijective assignment).
:- #count{P, Pos: player_year_position(P, Y, Pos)} != 1, years(Y).

% Ensure each position is used exactly once (bijective assignment).
:- #count{P, Y: player_year_position(P, Y, Pos)} != 1, positions(Pos).
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.

% For each person, there is exactly one drink and one price associated.

```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
diplomat("Fitzgerald"; "Howell"; "Riggs"; "Vasquez").
month(1; 2; 3; 4).
duration(2; 6; 8; 9).

% Single predicate to define the relation among diplomat, month, and duration.
% This captures the assignment of each diplomat to a unique month and duration.
assign(D, M, Dur) :- diplomat(D), month(M), duration(Dur).

% Ensure each diplomat is assigned exactly one month and one duration.
{assign(D, M, Dur): month(M), duration(Dur)}=1 :- diplomat(D).

% Ensure each month is used exactly once.
:- month(M), {assign(D, M, Dur): diplomat(D), duration(Dur)} != 1.

% Ensure each duration is used exactly once.
:- duration(Dur), {assign(D, M, Dur): diplomat(D), month(M)} != 1.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
```
```asp
winners("Daniel"; "Gabriel"; "Roland"; "Vincent").
price(250; 260; 270; 280).
order(3; 4; 7; 8).

% Define the single predicate to model the auction assignment: 
% auction(W, P, O) means winner W, price P, and order O for the same item.
auction(W, P, O) :- winners(W), price(P), order(O).

% Each winner, price, and order is used exactly once (one-to-one matching).
% Ensure each winner is assigned exactly one price and one order.
{auction(W, P, O) : price(P), order(O)} = 1 :- winners(W).

% Ensure each price is assigned to exactly one winner and one order.
{auction(W, P, O) : winners(W), order(O)} = 1 :- price(P).

% Ensure each order is assigned to exactly one winner and one price.
{auction(W, P, O) : winners(W), price(P)} = 1 :- order(O).

% Clue 1: The butterfly auctioned 7th sold for 20 less than the one won by Daniel.
% So: auction(W1, P1, 7) and auction(Daniel, P2, O2) with P1 = P2 - 20.
:- auction(_, P1, 7), auction("Daniel", P2, _), P1 != P2 - 20.

% Clue 2: The four butterflies are: 
% - the one sold for $260
% - the one auctioned 4th
% - the one auctioned 7th
% - the one won by Roland
% These are all distinct items → so the item with price 260 ≠ item with order 4, 7, or winner Roland, etc.
% We enforce that the item with price 260 is not the same as the one with order 4, 7, or winner Roland.
% Similarly, the item with order 4 is not the one with price 260 or winner Roland, etc.
% But since we have one-to-one matching, we can enforce that:
% - The winner of the $260 item ≠ Roland
% - The order of the $260 item ≠ 4, 7
% - The winner of the 4th item ≠ Roland
% - The order of the 4th item ≠ 260
% - The winner of the 7th item ≠ Roland
% - The order of the 7th item ≠ 260
% - The winner of Roland ≠ 260, 4, 7
% But we can express this more directly via constraints:
% The item with price 260 must not be the one with order 4, 7, or winner Roland.
:- auction(W, 260, O), W = "Roland.
:- auction(W, 260, 4).
:- auction(W, 260, 7).

% The item with order 4 must not be the one with price 260 or winner Roland.
:- auction(W, P, 4), W = "Roland".
:- auction(W, 260, 4).

% The item with order 7 must not be the one with price 260 or winner Roland.
:- auction(W, 260, 7).
:- auction(W, P, 7), W = "Roland".

% The item won by Roland must not be the one with price 260, order 4, or order 7.
:- auction("Roland", 260, O).
:- auction("Roland", P, 4).
:- auction("Roland", P, 7).

% Clue 3: The butterfly won by Gabriel was the 8th lot.
:- auction("Gabriel", P, O), O != 8.
```

```asp
% Define the constants in each category.
```
```asp
rockets("Beritron"; "Exatris"; "Foltron"; "Worul").
months(1; 2; 3; 4).

% Define the launch relation: launches_in(R, M) means rocket R launches in month M.

:- launches_in("Worul", M), launches_in("Exatris", M1), M1 != M + 1, months(M), months(M1).
```
